records.ety: [L. dis- + integratus, p. p. of integrare to renew, repair, fr. integer entire, whole. See Integer.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disintegrated; p. pr. & vb. n. Disintegrating.]

defs: To separate into integrant parts; to reduce to fragments or to powder; to break up, or cause to fall to pieces, as a rock, by blows of a hammer, frost, rain, and other mechanical or atmospheric influences.
quotes: Marlites are not disintegrated by exposure to the atmosphere, at least in six years.

defs: To decompose into integrant parts; as, chalk rapidly disintegrates.
